title: A Reproducible, Offline Computational Framework for Proteome-Scale Antidiabetic Peptide Discovery with Quantified Predictor Length Bias — Moso Bamboo (Phyllostachys edulis) Case Study
description: Scripts, benchmark datasets, and intermediate/final data products for a fully offline, reproducible computational framework that mines antidiabetic (DPP4-inhibitory) peptides at proteome scale from the moso bamboo (Phyllostachys edulis) proteome. Includes: offline reproductions of iDPPIV-SCM and iDPPIV-SI-style predictors; quantitative exposure and Tier0 length-matched debiasing of predictor length bias; multi-target (DPP4/ACE/alpha-glucosidase) same-proteome prioritization; genome-existence mapping and edible-shoot transcriptome bridging; AutoDock Vina docking and pocket-restricted MM-GBSA. No external API or online-service dependency.
